test.observation: 25 tests passed across all 4 workspace members in 0.05s

typecheck.observation: All checks passed (after fixing tomllib→tomli import in test files)

appliedUpdates.description: Added --index-url flags to install_rocm and install_cuda commands (rocm6.2 and cu124 respectively) so they install actual GPU torch wheels instead of CPU-only
Added uv 0.9.0 quirks section to environment.md: deprecated dev-dependencies syntax, conflicting-index limitation, tomllib vs tomli for Python 3.10

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update Dependencies section to reflect actual implementation: single PyTorch index with optional extras + conflicts, not per-member --index-url. Also note uv 0.9.0 deprecation of [tool.uv] dev-dependencies in favor of [dependency-groups].
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviewers flagged that AGENTS.md still references per-member index URLs (rocm6.2, cu124) which doesn't match the actual implementation. The actual approach uses a single generic index + optional-dependencies + [tool.uv] conflicts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update GPU dependency step to reflect the actual working approach: optional-dependencies + [tool.uv] conflicts instead of per-member sub-indexes. Also clarify TDD scope — config-only features (like workspace-root-config) don't need test files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker for workspace-member-pyprojects had to deviate from the skill's GPU dependency procedure due to uv workspace limitations. Worker for workspace-root-config correctly skipped TDD for config-only work, which the skill implies but doesn't make explicit.

rejectedObservations.observation: uv workspace conflicting-index limitation knowledge should be preserved
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented: architecture.md 'GPU Dependency Management' section already documents this limitation and the workaround in detail
